# Cobra Source Reference

> Cobra is a Go library for building command-line interfaces with nested commands, flags, validation, shell completion, and generated documentation.

## Guide

- [Cobra Source Reference](/guide.html): Cobra is a Go library for building command-line interfaces with nested commands, flags, validation, shell completion, and generated documentation.
- [Source Report](/guide/source-report.html): The site uses Sourcey `3.6.3` with a Go API snapshot generated by:
- [Command Model](/guide/command-model.html): Cobra centers CLI behavior on the `Command` type. A command holds user-facing metadata, relationships to parent and child commands, execution hooks, help and usage behavior, and flag sets.
- [Flags And Validation](/guide/flags-and-validation.html): Cobra uses `pflag` for command flags and adds helpers for argument counts, required flags, mutually exclusive groups, and related flag groups.
- [Completion And Documentation Generation](/guide/completion-and-docgen.html): Cobra ships support for shell completion and command documentation generation. These APIs are public because many CLI projects expose completion or docs commands as part of their release workflow.
- [github.com/spf13/cobra](/guide/api-cobra-package.html): Package cobra is a commander providing a simple interface to create powerful modern CLI interfaces. In addition to providing an interface, Cobra simultaneously provides a controller to organize your application code.
- [github.com/spf13/cobra/doc](/guide/api-doc-package.html): GenManHeader is a lot like the .TH header at the start of man pages. These include the title, section, date, source, and manual. We will use the current time if Date is unset and will use "Auto generated by spf13/cobra" if the Source is unset.
